title: Day 16 Quiz — Redux Toolkit and Pure Functions
description: Verify Redux Toolkit concepts, pure functions, slice typing, and immutable updates.

questions.prompt: Redux reducers must be pure functions that do not mutate their arguments.
questions.explanation: Pure reducers enable predictable updates, DevTools time travel, and testability.

questions.prompt: What does createSlice from Redux Toolkit generate?

options.text: Reducer, action creators, and action types

questions.explanation: createSlice bundles reducers with auto-generated action creators and action type strings.

options.text: Once at app setup, passed to Provider

questions.explanation: One store per app, provided at root via react-redux Provider.

questions.prompt: Which are advantages of Redux Toolkit over hand-written Redux? Select all that apply.

options.text: Replaces the need for React components

questions.explanation: RTK reduces boilerplate, includes Immer, and configures store defaults. It does not replace React.

questions.prompt: Derive typed RootState with {{blank1}} and typed dispatch with {{blank2}} from the configured store.
questions.explanation: These type exports power typed useAppSelector and useAppDispatch hooks.

questions.prompt: With Immer inside createSlice, what happens to state.items.push()?
questions.explanation: Immer drafts produce immutable next state despite mutating syntax — the correct mental answer is immutable update is produced.

questions.prompt: When is Redux Toolkit preferred over Context from Day 15?

options.text: Complex client state with many actions and DevTools needs

questions.explanation: RTK shines for complex mutable client state; Context fits simpler global preferences.
RootState types the selector state parameter.

questions.prompt: Why must reducer functions be pure?
questions.sampleAnswer: Pure reducers return the same output for the same input without side effects or mutating arguments. This makes state predictable, enables Redux DevTools time travel, and simplifies unit testing.
questions.explanation: Purity is foundational to Redux architecture.

questions.prompt: What hook connects React components to the Redux store for reading state?
questions.explanation: useSelector (or typed useAppSelector) subscribes to store slices.
